hierarchy.name: Registration & Registry
hierarchy.desc: Property transactions require mandatory registration at the Sub-Registrar's office under the Registration Act to ensure legal validity.

hierarchy.name: Sale Deed & Gift Deed
hierarchy.desc: Drafting and executing valid transfer documents. A Sale Deed transfers ownership for a price, while a Gift Deed transfers it out of love and affection.

hierarchy.name: Mutation & Land Records
hierarchy.desc: Updating revenue records (Khata/Patta) after property transfer to ensure property tax is assessed in the new owner's name.

hierarchy.name: Partition
hierarchy.desc: Dividing ancestral or jointly owned property among co-owners through a deed of partition or a partition suit in a civil court.

hierarchy.name: Illegal Possession & Encroachment
hierarchy.desc: Legal remedies under the Specific Relief Act and criminal complaints to reclaim property from squatters or encroachers.

hierarchy.name: Common Disputes
hierarchy.desc: Resolving boundary disputes, contested titles, inheritance conflicts, and builder-buyer issues under RERA.

hierarchy.name: Contracts & Labour Rights
hierarchy.desc: Understanding the terms of employment, non-compete clauses, working hours, and fundamental rights under the Industrial Disputes Act.

hierarchy.name: Salary Issues & Notice Period
hierarchy.desc: Addressing unpaid wages, unauthorized deductions, and enforcing notice period clauses for both employer and employee.

hierarchy.name: PF & ESI
hierarchy.desc: Ensuring employer compliance with Provident Fund (PF) and Employee State Insurance (ESI) contributions, and resolving withdrawal issues.

hierarchy.name: Workplace Harassment
hierarchy.desc: Filing complaints under the POSH Act with the Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) for safe working conditions.

hierarchy.name: Wrongful Termination
hierarchy.desc: Legal recourse against illegal retrenchment, dismissal without adequate cause, or forced resignation (constructive dismissal).

hierarchy.name: Marriage
hierarchy.desc: Requirements for a valid marriage under personal laws or the Special Marriage Act, and mandatory registration.

hierarchy.name: Divorce
hierarchy.desc: Grounds for contested divorce or mutual consent divorce, including mediation and the cooling-off period.

hierarchy.name: Maintenance
hierarchy.desc: Claiming financial support (alimony) for spouse, children, or parents under personal laws or Section 125 of the CrPC.

hierarchy.name: Domestic Violence
hierarchy.desc: Filing complaints under the Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act (PWDVA) for protection, residence, and monetary orders.

hierarchy.name: Child Custody & Adoption
hierarchy.desc: Determining physical/legal custody based on child welfare, and the legal process for adoption under CARA guidelines.

hierarchy.name: Succession & Inheritance
hierarchy.desc: Rules for intestate succession (dying without a will), drafting a valid will, and obtaining a succession certificate.

hierarchy.name: Institution of Suit
hierarchy.desc: Filing the plaint with appropriate court fees. First-instance institution of monetary, recovery, or property lawsuits.

hierarchy.name: District Court (First Appeal)
hierarchy.desc: Appellate review of decrees passed by sub-judges and trial of suits exceeding municipal valuation limits.

hierarchy.name: High Court (Second Appeal)
hierarchy.desc: Second appeals on substantial questions of law, writ petitions, and constitutional oversight.

hierarchy.name: Supreme Court
hierarchy.desc: Apex adjudication, federal arbitration, and final interpretation of the Constitution via Special Leave Petition (SLP).

hierarchy.name: Magistrate Court
hierarchy.desc: Filing of charge-sheets, bail hearings, summary trials for minor offenses, and committing sessions-level crimes.

hierarchy.name: Sessions Court
hierarchy.desc: Trial of serious crimes (homicide, rape) and first appeal hearings from magistrate courts.

hierarchy.name: High Court
hierarchy.desc: Writ petitions for quashing FIRs, bail applications, and death sentence confirmations.

hierarchy.name: Supreme Court
hierarchy.desc: Apex judicial review, final sentencing appeals, and constitutional integrity verification.
